patch.value:
## Context

File: src/cli/commands/telemetry.ts:199-253 — buildTelemetryStatsBuckets. Currently tracks count, max_attempts, event_type_counts, schema versions. Fields ok and command_resolution from command_finish payload (runtime.ts:927-930) are not extracted.

File: src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts:927 — command_resolution emitted in command_finish payload at all capture levels.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each bucket gains: ok_count, error_count (derived from command_finish ok field); error_rate (0–1 float, absent when no finish events); command_resolution_counts (map of resolution -> count).
- ok_count + error_count == number of command_finish events in that bucket.
- pm telemetry stats output includes these fields.
- Unit tests assert correct tallying and that missing/malformed payload.ok is treated as not-ok (conservative).

## Evidence

src/cli/commands/telemetry.ts:211 — eventType is parsed; payload fields are not.
src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts:927 — command_resolution present in finish payload at all capture levels.
pm telemetry stats: add error_rate and resolution breakdown per command bucket

pm telemetry stats buckets commands by count and event_type_counts (command_start / command_finish) but does not surface success/failure rates or command_resolution distribution within the local queue. This makes it impossible to detect a degraded-error-rate locally without querying the remote stack. command_finish events already carry ok (bool) and command_resolution fields.
pm telemetry stats output shows ok_count, error_count, error_rate, command_resolution_counts per bucket; tests cover all capture_level variants (minimal has only resolution, not ok field directly).

- Each bucket gains: ok_count, error_count (derived from command_finish ok field); error_rate (0–1 float, absent when no finish events); command_resolution_counts (map of resolution -> count).
- ok_count + error_count == number of command_finish events in that bucket.
- pm telemetry stats output includes these fields.
- Unit tests assert correct tallying and that missing/malformed payload.ok is treated as not-ok (conservative).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Bundled with pm-3n3b/pm-fbyu in one PR (epic pm-5oj5). ok_count/error_count/error_rate (conservative: missing/non-true ok counts as error) + command_resolution_counts derived from command_finish payloads. ok_count+error_count == finish-event count by construction. Coverage 100/100/100/100.

patch.value: pm telemetry stats now reports ok_count/error_count/error_rate and command_resolution_counts per command bucket from command_finish payloads; missing/non-true ok counted conservatively as error.
Shipped local error-rate + resolution breakdown in telemetry stats with full unit coverage; 100/100/100/100.
Each bucket reports ok_count/error_count/error_rate and command_resolution_counts from command_finish payloads.
Conservative ok handling and resolution tally verified; ok_count+error_count==finish count; 100/100/100/100.
